boundary_binding.action_boundary: May facilities allow contractor site work to begin?
boundary_binding.allow_rule_assessment: All required terms exist in source records (Iris Installers, Site 4Q, 2026-06-28, umbrella liability, currently bound CE-12) and END-IRI-089 confirms effective status before work begins.
boundary_binding.controlling_source_fact: STD-IRI-089-P requires insurance-status class verification before work begins. WARN-IRI-089 blocks this verification. END-IRI-089 provides coverage authority but does not resolve the status-class warning.
boundary_binding.escalate_rule_assessment: WARN-IRI-089 explicitly requires separate insurance-status verification. The warning is not resolved by endorsement presence. The policy (STD-IRI-089-P) requires insurance-status class confirmation before execution.
boundary_binding.timing_scope_authority_dependency_check: Gov baton final_compiler_allowed=false conflicts with worker_role=FINAL_COMPILER assignment. Unresolved conflict prevents closure.

critical_features_preserved: Source boundary preserved - no external sources added
All prior gate failures preserved for repair
final_answer: The source records contain all required terms (Iris Installers, Site 4Q, umbrella liability, CE-12, effective before work begins), and END-IRI-089 provides coverage authority. However, WARN-IRI-089 explicitly requires separate insurance-status verification that is not resolved by endorsement presence. The policy (STD-IRI-089-P) requires insurance-status class confirmation before execution. The action cannot proceed because the warning-class dependency remains open. Additionally, the Gov baton sets final_compiler_allowed=false while assigning FINAL_COMPILER role—this conflict must be resolved before the action can be allowed.
open_blockers: Gov baton conflict: final_compiler_allowed=false vs worker_role=FINAL_COMPILER - flag for resolution
STD-IRI-089-P status-class requirement not satisfied before execution
